one_liner: Strong discovery with a clearly committed next step.
rationale: The rep led with open-ended discovery, quantified the prospect's pain in time and dollars, and locked in a concrete follow-up by Friday. Budget was not confirmed.
next_step_detail: Send proposal by Friday and schedule follow-up demo with the technical lead.

objection_handling.summary: Acknowledged the existing vendor relationship and pivoted to renewal timing rather than fighting on price.

objections.objection: We already have a vendor for this.
objections.response_summary: Acknowledged the relationship and probed renewal date.

evidence.speaker: Rep
evidence.quote: Totally understand — when is that contract up for renewal?

strengths.title: Open-ended discovery
strengths.detail: Surfaced pain through genuine curiosity rather than leading with the pitch.

evidence.speaker: Rep
evidence.quote: Walk me through what your team's typical week looks like.

strengths.title: Quantified impact
strengths.detail: Translated process inefficiency into concrete hours and dollars per quarter.

evidence.speaker: Rep
evidence.quote: So that's roughly 12 hours a week — about $4k a quarter.

opportunities.title: Skipped budget check
opportunities.detail: Never confirmed whether budget exists for this initiative this quarter.
opportunities.suggestion: Add a budget-availability question before sending the proposal.

evidence.speaker: Rep
evidence.quote: Let's talk pricing on the next call.

opportunities.title: Filler words during pricing
opportunities.detail: Frequent 'um' and 'like' during the pricing discussion undercut authority.
opportunities.suggestion: Pause silently instead of filling — projects confidence.

evidence.speaker: Rep
evidence.quote: Um, so, like, the pricing — um — kind of depends.
